<!--
  AUDIENCE: Engineers, AI agents, and consumers tracking what changed between versions.
  PURPOSE:  Provide a human-readable history of all notable changes. Append only — never
            edit or delete past entries.
  LOCATION: CHANGELOG.md (repository root)
  TEMPLATE: docs/templates/changelog.template.md
  FORMAT:   Based on https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/
            Versioning follows https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html
-->

# Changelog

All notable changes to [REQUIRED: project or component name] will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

---

## [Unreleased]

[REQUIRED: List changes that are merged but not yet released. Move to a versioned section on release.]

### Added

- [REQUIRED: new feature or document — phrase as "Added X to Y"]

### Changed

- [OPTIONAL: change to existing behaviour — phrase as "Changed X from A to B"]

### Fixed

- [OPTIONAL: bug fix — phrase as "Fixed X which caused Y"]

### Deprecated

- [OPTIONAL: feature marked for removal in a future release]

### Removed

- [OPTIONAL: feature removed in this release]

### Security

- [OPTIONAL: security fix — always include CVE or reference if applicable]

---

## [OPTIONAL: version — e.g. 1.0.0] — [YYYY-MM-DD]

[OPTIONAL: One sentence summarising what this release represents.]

### Added

- [entry]

### Changed

- [entry]

### Fixed

- [entry]

---

<!--
  CHANGELOG RULES:
  - Every entry MUST use one of the six section headings above (Added, Changed, Fixed,
    Deprecated, Removed, Security). Do not invent new section names.
  - Entries MUST be written in the past tense, imperative voice:
      "Added X" not "Adds X"
      "Fixed Y" not "Fixes Y"
  - Entries MUST NOT reference internal ticket numbers only — include a brief
    human-readable description of the change.
  - NEVER edit or delete past entries. Corrections to past entries are new entries
    in [Unreleased] with a note referencing the entry being corrected.
  - Breaking changes MUST be flagged with **BREAKING:** at the start of the entry.
-->
